items.title: Ukraine rescuers pull dead from rubble after Russian strikes kill 18 people
items.summary: An eight-year-old boy and a woman killed in a strike on an apartment block are among the dead, officials say.

items.title: Clashes continue in Lebanon despite Israel and Hezbollah accepting US partial ceasefire plan
items.summary: A cessation of hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah is seen as crucial to the peace process with Iran.

items.title: Two people shot dead amid Kenya protests against US Ebola quarantine centre plan
items.summary: The US plan has sparked public anger and led to demonstrations near the site of the proposed treatment facility.

items.title: Instagram AI chatbot tricked by hackers to give access to others' accounts
items.summary: Some reports have linked the incident to recent cases of high-profile Instagram accounts being hijacked.

items.title: Denmark's Mette Frederiksen to form government after months of negotiations
items.summary: The leader of Denmark's Social Democratic Party is forming a centre-left coalition minority government, giving her a third term as prime minister.

items.title: Sabrina Carpenter gets restraining order against man who showed up at her home
items.summary: The singer reported a series of "deeply alarming" incidents at her house to Los Angeles police.

items.title: China goes after 'ghost kitchens' to rein in cut-throat food delivery apps
items.summary: The thousands of "ghost kitchens" - online shops that don't actually exist - have spooked Chinese consumers.

items.title: Trump administration will abide by ruling halting $1.8bn 'anti-weaponisation' fund
items.summary: The justice department says it  "disagrees strongly" with a court decision to halt the establishment of the fund.

items.title: Australian ex-minister launches crowd-funded inquiry into Aukus submarine deal
items.summary: Inquiry will look at whether Australia will receive the submarines and whether it will make the country safer.

items.title: Missing lab worker found dead in New Mexico nearly a year after disappearing
items.summary: Melissa Casias' case was part of debunked speculations about recent deaths and disappearances in the US scientific community.

items.title: AI giant Anthropic plans to sell shares in US as valuation nears $1tn
items.summary: The AI company behind Claude is set to offer the public the chance to buy and sell shares in the firm later this year.

items.title: Italians bemused by Milan bull mosaic restoration
items.summary: Italians wondered what had happened to the animal's "lucky-charm" testicles.

items.title: Six people killed in Iowa domestic dispute, police say
items.summary: The suspected shooter was found dead near a bridge with a self-inflicted gunshot wound, authorities say.

items.title: Three Ebola vaccines in development amid growing outbreak fears
items.summary: IAVI, Moderna and the University of Oxford are all working on new vaccines.

items.title: OpenAI let ChatGPT aid and abet mass shooters, Florida lawsuit claims
items.summary: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier alleges OpenAI and boss Sam Altman built a 'web of deceit'

items.title: Litterbugs now face on-the-spot fines in Tokyo's tourist hotspot
items.summary: Dozens of officials will patrol world-famous Shibuya as Japan responds to the impacts of overtourism.

items.title: He was a reality show villain. Can he be the mayor of one of America's largest cities?
items.summary: Republican Spencer Pratt, known for his TV role on The Hills, appears to be in a tight race with two Democrats.

items.title: Why Myanmar president's India visit is being closely watched
items.summary: Myanmar is seeking to broaden diplomatic engagement after years of international criticism and isolation.

items.title: Cara Delevingne says new music was inspired by getting sober
items.summary: The model and actress is now moving into the world of music, having released her first two songs.

items.title: Toy Story 5 shows 'terror' of children's screen addiction, says Tom Hanks
items.summary: The latest film sees Woody, Buzz and the other toys compete with a new tablet for children's attention.

items.title: Bowen: Trump needs this war to end but Iran is not backing down
items.summary: Under pressure from the polls and Gulf allies, the White House is pushing for a deal but Iran wants concessions, writes BBC's international editor.

items.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
items.summary: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

items.title: How health workers in DR Congo are treating Ebola and staying safe
items.summary: There are currently no approved drugs that target Bundibugyo - the species of Ebola responsible for this outbreak.

items.title: Watch: Explosion at fireworks factory in Malta
items.summary: Two men were injured in the explosion and were taken to hospital with minor injuries.

items.title: Watch: Glass doors break as fans scramble to see Pursuit of Jade star
items.summary: Huge crowds formed outside a shopping centre in China to see actor Zhang Linghe.

items.title: Marilyn Monroe 'lookalikes' gather to celebrate her 100th birthday
items.summary: Fans set a new world record for the largest gathering of people dressed as Marilyn Monroe to celebrate what would have been the legendary actress’ centenary.

items.title: Between celebration and confrontation: Paris after PSG victory
items.summary: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police following the Champions League final.
